output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Get It Back in 60 Seconds
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights and send a legally demanding letter in 60 seconds for just $19.99. No signup needed.
content_outline: The Real Reason Landlords Withhold Security Deposits (And Get Away With It)
What Your Landlord Can and Cannot Legally Deduct
How to Respond When Your Deposit Isn't Returned
The Fastest Way to Demand Your Deposit Back: A 60-Second Solution
State-by-State Deadlines: How Long Landlords Have to Return Your Money
key_points: Most landlords withhold deposits hoping tenants won't fight back because hiring a lawyer costs too much
Normal wear and tear, carpet cleaning, and repainting cannot be deducted from your security deposit in most states
Deposits-Back.com lets you generate a legally sound demand letter in 60 seconds for a one-time $19.99 fee with no subscription or account required
Sending a formal demand letter shows you know your rights and often forces immediate repayment without going to court
Each state has specific deadlines ranging from 14 to 60 days for landlords to return deposits or provide an itemized deduction list
output.cta: Don't let your landlord keep what's yours. Generate your deposit demand letter in 60 seconds for $19.99—no signup, no subscription, just results.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlords are withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposits and want a fast, affordable legal solution without hiring an attorney
social_hooks: POV: You moved out 60 days ago and your landlord just texted 'the check is in the mail' for the 4th time
My landlord tried to charge me $800 for a 'paint touch-up.' I sent one letter and got every penny back.
Your landlord legally cannot keep your deposit for normal wear and tear. Most tenants don't know this.
Stop arguing with your landlord in the group chat. Here's how I got my $2,400 deposit back in 5 days with one letter.
Landlords bank on you being too tired to fight. This $19.99 letter changes everything—no lawyer needed.
If your landlord hasn't returned your deposit within 30 days, they're probably breaking the law. Do this next.
I almost paid $300 for a lawyer to get my deposit back. Then I found this 60-second hack.
Security deposit horror story: They charged me for 'dust.' The dispute letter I sent shut that down immediately.
